page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the N2 family. It is configured with 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz. The instance features 768 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. It operates on the x86_64 architecture and does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The instance achieved a strong rating in multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench. Memory bandwidth tests demonstrate high efficiency, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4896.56 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 144.43 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also registered strong performance, processing 88.47 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU tasks and large-scale LLM text generation show average to poor performance, reflecting a tradeoff where parallelized workloads are favored over single-threaded execution speed.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation make this instance suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without the overhead of bare-metal deployments. It is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput static web serving, and parallelized software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with dedicated CPU allocation, this instance provides 8 GB of memory per vCPU but does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database operation throughput, though single-core performance remains average. This resource profile makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, including large databases, in-memory caches, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and high memory bandwidth for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM providing 8 GB per core
Dedicated 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor architecture
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Optimized for large databases and in-memory caches
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
